expectations.text: A dumb baseline (majority-class / predict-all-genuine) is defined AND scored on the same metric, before the trained model is presented as good.
expectations.evidence: 04-baseline.md: majority-class 'predict Class=0' baseline scored at PR-AUC = base rate 0.00167 (ROC-AUC 0.5), before /ds-model.

expectations.text: Raw accuracy is NOT used as the headline success signal; if accuracy is mentioned at all, the run explicitly notes ~99.83% is the no-skill majority-class rate, not evidence of a good model.
expectations.evidence: 04-baseline.md: states majority-class accuracy would be 99.83% and is 'actively misleading'; accuracy is reported nowhere.

expectations.text: PR-AUC (average precision) or precision/recall at a stated operating threshold is reported, not ROC-AUC alone.
expectations.evidence: 07-evaluate.md: headline metric PR-AUC 0.845; ROC-AUC 0.980 kept only as a secondary check.

expectations.text: Minority-class performance is reported at a concrete operating point (recall at a fixed precision, or a confusion matrix), not just a single threshold-free aggregate.
expectations.evidence: 07-evaluate.md: frozen operating threshold 0.4932, recall 83.1%, with 80 missed-fraud and 51 false-alarm cases enumerated.

expectations.text: The validation split is stratified to preserve the ~0.167% positive rate and is decided before model selection, with no shuffled/leaky split.
expectations.evidence: 05-validate.md: StratifiedKFold(5) verified to preserve the 0.167% fraud rate per fold; dedup before the split; chosen before /ds-model.

expectations.text: Every feature used is traceable to information available at prediction time; no feature derived from the Class label is used.
expectations.evidence: 00-frame/03-prep: features are PCA components V1-V28 plus Amount/Time; no feature derived from the Class label.

expectations.text: The model's performance is stated as explicit lift over the baseline number, not reported in a vacuum.
expectations.evidence: 04/07: PR-AUC 0.845 stated as a large lift over the 0.00167 no-skill baseline, not in a vacuum.

expectations.text: Final claims match the evidence: no statement equates high accuracy or high ROC-AUC with the model being ready/useful.
expectations.evidence: 07-evaluate.md: no accuracy-equals-ready claim; top-decile over-confidence and small-dollar missed-fraud limitation disclosed.

notes: REAL run: graded against the shipped skilled pipeline in benchmarks/<dataset>/.last-ds-mile/stages/.
